---
title: "OLA Ride Analysis Dashboard"
author: "Meeval Augustine"
date: "2024-06-10"
description: "A comprehensive Power BI dashboard for Ola ride analysis, tracking over 93.61K bookings."
categories: [Power BI, MySQL, Performance Analysis, DAX]
image: ../../images/ola_thumb.png
toc: false
---

## Project Overview

<a href="https://github.com/Meeval20/OLA-RIDE-ANALYSIS" target="_blank" class="cv-download-btn" style="margin-bottom: 1.5rem;">
  {{< fa brands github >}} &nbsp; View Full Project on GitHub
</a>

Developed a comprehensive **Power BI** dashboard for Ola ride analysis, tracking over **93.61K bookings** and **$33.46M in booking value** to provide key business insights.

**Key Deliverables:**
- Used **MySQL** queries for fast retrieval of data for stakeholder requirements on fast decision making.
- Analyzed and visualized ride-sharing performance by vehicle type, identifying **Prime Plus** as the top revenue generator with **$4.84M** in booking value.
- Built a detailed cancellation analysis module, highlighting that **19%** of rides were customer-canceled versus **3.39%** driver-canceled.
- Monitored and reported on booking success rates (**61.88%**) and failure rates (**38.12%**).
- Designed series of visuals including line charts for daily trends and pie charts for segmentation.

## Demonstrating Success Rates with Python

To satisfy the Quarto environment requirements, I am demonstrating the Booking Success vs Failure rate logic using an embedded Python chunk.

```{python}
#| label: fig-ola-success
#| fig-cap: OLA Ride Booking Outcomes

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

labels = ['Success Rate', 'Failure Rate']
rates = [61.88, 38.12]
colors = ['#27ae60', '#e74c3c']

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 4))
bars = ax.barh(labels, rates, color=colors, edgecolor='black')

# Labeling bars
for bar in bars:
    width = bar.get_width()
    ax.text(width + 1, bar.get_y() + bar.get_height()/2, 
            f'{width}%', ha='left', va='center', weight='bold')

ax.set_xlim(0, 80)
ax.set_xlabel('Percentage (%)')
ax.set_title('Ride Booking Success vs Failure Rates', fontsize=14, pad=15)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

This visualization reinforces the finding that there is a significant margin for improving operational efficiency by targeting the 38.12% failure segment.
